title: A Warning To Hong Kong’s Gray Market
text:
A Warning To Hong Kong’s Gray Market

Xiaoxi Zhang, Wei He

Gavekal Dragonomics | 11 Jun 2026

When Chinese regulators in late May launched a crackdown on some Hong Kong-based online brokers, the news set off tremors throughout the offshore financial industry. It’s an open secret that much of Hong Kong’s financial sector relies on Chinese residents’ desire to move money outside the mainland’s strict capital controls. The incentive to hold assets outside of China has only intensified in the past couple of years, as global tech stocks have surged and domestic interest rates have ground lower. The brokerages Futu, Tiger Brokers and Longbridge helped meet that demand, but have now been fined and punished for soliciting customers in mainland China without an onshore license.
